vehicle.label: Vehicle business-use
vehicle.hint: Business-use % = business km / total km. CRA requires a logbook to support your claim.

home_office.label: Home office
home_office.hint: Office % = office area / total home area. Space must be used regularly and exclusively for business, or be your principal place of business.

line_mappings.hint: All business income from clients. Zero-rated if client is outside Canada.
Miscellaneous business income not from direct client work.
Online ads, marketing materials, promotional costs.
Only 50% of meal/entertainment expenses are deductible. ITC/ITR also limited to 50%.
Pens, paper, printer ink, desk accessories, minor office items.
Software subscriptions, SaaS tools, cloud services used for business.
Web hosting, cloud infrastructure, domain registration.
Subcontractor and freelancer payments.
Accountant, lawyer, bookkeeper fees.
Flights, hotels, car rentals for business travel. Does not include daily commute.
Postage, courier, shipping costs for business.
Major equipment purchases. May need to be capitalized and claimed as CCA over multiple years rather than expensed in full.
Coworking space or dedicated business office rent. Home office rent uses proration instead.
Business phone line, business portion of mobile plan.
Business account fees, service charges, transaction fees. Not GST/QST eligible.
Business expenses that do not fit other categories.
Tax filing software, accountant fees for business return preparation.
Gas, diesel. Deductible portion based on business-use %.
Car insurance premiums. Not subject to GST/QST. Deductible portion based on business-use %.
Oil changes, tires, repairs. Deductible portion based on business-use %.
Lease payments or interest on vehicle loan (not principal). Deductible portion based on business-use %.
Business-related parking. Deductible portion based on business-use %.
Electricity bill. Deductible portion based on home office %.
Natural gas or heating fuel. Deductible portion based on home office %.
Mortgage INTEREST only (not principal). Deductible portion based on home office %. Not subject to GST/QST.
Municipal/school property tax. Deductible portion based on home office %. Not subject to GST/QST.
Homeowner's insurance premium. Deductible portion based on home office %. Not subject to GST/QST.
Home internet service. Deductible portion based on home office %.
Home repairs and maintenance. Deductible portion based on home office %.

reminders.text: Keep all receipts for 6 years from the end of the tax year. CRA can request them at any time during an audit.
CRA requires a logbook to support your business-use percentage claim. Record date, destination, purpose, and kilometres for every business trip.
Only 50% of meal and entertainment expenses are deductible. The GST/QST ITC/ITR is also limited to 50%.
Your home office must be used regularly and exclusively for business, OR be your principal place of business (where you earn more than 50% of income).
Services provided to non-resident clients (e.g., US companies) are zero-rated for GST/QST. You do not charge GST/QST but can still claim ITCs/ITRs on business expenses.
Claim ITCs/ITRs in the reporting period when the expense was paid or became payable, not when the return is filed.
In the year you acquire a capital asset, you can only claim half the normal CCA rate (half-year rule). Consider immediate expensing rules for eligible property.
Only the interest portion of your mortgage payment is deductible as a home office expense. Principal payments are not deductible.

info_sections.title: T2125 -- Statement of Business or Professional Activities
info_sections.body: Federal form for reporting self-employment income and expenses. Filed as part of your personal T1 return. Reports gross income, allowable expenses by category, net income, and business-use-of-home and motor vehicle expenses. All amounts in CAD.

info_sections.title: TP-80 -- Business or Professional Income and Expenses
info_sections.body: Quebec equivalent of the federal T2125. Filed with your TP-1 provincial return. Same income and expense structure but with Quebec-specific line numbers. Amounts must match your T2125 figures.

info_sections.title: GST/QST Returns and Input Tax Credits
info_sections.body: As a registered business, you file GST/QST returns (annually, quarterly, or monthly depending on revenue). Even if your income is zero-rated (export services), you must file returns. You can claim Input Tax Credits (ITCs for GST) and Input Tax Refunds (ITRs for QST) on eligible business expenses. ITCs/ITRs are computed by applying the tax rate to the pre-tax amount of eligible expenses.

info_sections.title: Motor Vehicle Expense Deductions
info_sections.body: You can deduct vehicle expenses proportional to business use. Track total km driven in the year and business km driven. Eligible expenses: fuel, insurance, maintenance, lease payments or loan interest, licence and registration, parking. Keep a logbook.

info_sections.title: Business-Use-of-Home Deductions
info_sections.body: If you work from home, you can deduct a portion of household expenses. The portion is calculated as: office square footage / total home square footage. Eligible expenses: mortgage interest (not principal), property tax, home insurance, electricity, heating, water, internet, maintenance. Home office expenses cannot create or increase a business loss -- carry forward any excess to the next year.
